title: Determinants of price elasticity and the total revenue rule
description: Practice what you've learned about the relationship between price elasticity of demand and total revenue in this exercise.
topic_path: Determinants of price elasticity and the total revenue rule

assessment_items.question_content:
The price elasticity of demand for good X equals $0$.

**Which of the following is most likely to be a characteristic of good X?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It has no substitutes and is a necessity.
There is ample time to find alternatives to good X.
It makes up a large share of consumers’ budgets.
It is a luxury good.
There are many firms selling a product almost identical to it.
When there are few substitutes for a good
When buyers have a lot of time to shop around
When a market is narrowly defined
When a good is a luxury
When it makes up a large share of a person’s budget

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a good that is likely to have the *most* elastic demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A luxury with many substitutes
A necessity with a few substitutes
A broadly defined good, such as "food"
Goods that make up a small share of the budget
Goods that have to be bought under a short time constraint

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose a firm was interested in how much their revenue would change if they change the price of their good.

**Which of the following would a firm use to determine how its revenue changes based on the price elasticity of demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total profit test
Total revenue test
Price elasticity of supply
Income elasticity of demand
Cross-price elasticity of demand

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best description of the total revenue test?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: a way of using elasticity to predict changes in the money a firm takes in
a way of determining if two goods are complements or substitutes
a way of determining how much profit that a company earns.
a formula for calculating the total amount of money received by a firm.
a way to calculate the revenue received using price times quantity

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would be an indication that a firm has an elastic demand for its goods?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total revenue increases when it lowers its price
Consumer spending increases when it raises its price
Total revenue doesn’t change when it raises its price
Total revenue doesn’t change when it lowers its price
Consumer spending decreases when it lowers its price

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the price elasticity of demand for books is $5$, what happens if a bookseller increases the price of books?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bookseller’s total revenue decreases.
There is no change to the bookseller’s total revenue.
The price elasticity of demand will decrease.
The bookseller’s total revenue increases.
Consumer spending on books increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the price elasticity of demand for apples is $3$, then what will be the impact on total revenue if price increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total revenue will not change
Total revenue will increase
Total revenue will fall to zero
Total revenue will decrease
Total revenue triples

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a possible change in total revenue that occurs if you increase the price of a good with unit elasticity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these values of price elasticity will result in no change in total revenue if a firm increases its price?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\infty$

assessment_items.question_content:
The city of Xela raised the fees that it charged for dumping waste at the city dump, but the revenue the city earned from dump fees didn’t change. 

**What is the price elasticity of demand for dump fees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Unit elastic
Elastic
Inelastic
Perfectly elastic
Perfectly inelastic

assessment_items.question_content:
Susan's Shellfish Shack increased the price of their shrimp sandwiches by $10\%$, and her total revenue increased $20\%$.

**Based on this information, what can we conclude about shrimp sandwiches?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: Consumers consider shrimp sandwiches luxuries.
Consumers consider shrimp sandwiches necessities
Consumers have an infinite elasticity of demand for shrimp sandwiches.
Consumers have a unit elastic demand for shrimp sandwiches.
Consumers think shrimp sandwiches and crab sandwiches are substitutes

assessment_items.question_content:
Zikri wants to increase total revenue at his restaurant. The price elasticity of demand for several dishes that he serves are given in the table below.

|| **Price elasticity of demand of Malaysian dishes** ||
Dish | Price elasticity of demand
-|-
Nasi lemak | 0.7
Satay | 2
Rendang | 1
Ulam | 1.4
Pulut | 100

**What dish should Zikri increase the price of if he wants to increase total revenue?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nasi lemak
Satay
Rendang
Ulam
Pulut

assessment_items.question_content:
When the price of jackfruit decreased $50\%$, total revenues earned by jackfruit sellers decreased. 

**What can be concluded based on this information?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for jackfruit is price inelastic.
The price elasticity of demand for jackfruit is $1$.
The demand for jackfruit is elastic.
The demand for jackfruit is unit elastic
The price elasticity of demand for jackfruit is 5

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the total revenue rule, what happens when you raise the price on a good with inelastic demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total revenue increases.
Total revenue falls to zero.
Total revenue decreases.
Total revenue doesn’t change.
Consumer spending on the good stays the same
